headline: National Service Program Returns With RM1.2 Billion Budget
context: The revived PLKN 2.0 program was announced in late 2025 targeting 150,000 youth annually starting mid-2026.

cards.big: PLKN 2.0 allocates RM8,000 per participant for a 3-month program
cards.sub: Previous PLKN ran from 2004 to 2015 before being suspended

cards.big: Original PLKN saw 23 trainee deaths between 2004 and 2015
cards.sub: Safety concerns were a major factor in the program's suspension

cards.big: RM1.2 billion could fund 40,000 full TVET scholarships at current rates
cards.sub: Opportunity cost of national service versus skills training is significant

cards.big: South Korea and Singapore integrate national service with skills certification
cards.sub: Malaysia's PLKN 2.0 framework has no formal accreditation component

cards.big: National unity programs work only if outcomes are measured beyond attendance numbers
Should national service be voluntary with incentives rather than compulsory
